AURORAвҖҷS ROOM вҳ…

Steam still curled from the bathroom mirror when Aurora stepped out, her damp hair clinging to her neck. She wore nothing but a soft robe and a towel around her shoulders. The silence of the enormous room pressed in вҖ” elegant, yes, but coldly unfamiliar.

She padded barefoot toward the wardrobe and pulled it open. Empty. Not even a hanger inside.

A soft knock came at the door.

Aurora hesitated, then opened it to find the elderly woman from earlier standing there вҖ” her kind eyes and composed smile instantly recognizable.

вҖңI guessed you might not have brought anything,вҖқ the woman said warmly, holding out a neatly folded pile of clothes. вҖңSo I brought a few things for you, dear.вҖқ

Aurora accepted them quickly. вҖңThank you, maвҖҷam.вҖқ

вҖңItвҖҷs Nanny Celia,вҖқ the woman corrected gently.

вҖңThank you, Nanny Celia,вҖқ Aurora repeated, her tone polite but small.

вҖңGood. Dress up and come downstairs for dinner,вҖқ Celia said with a reassuring nod before leaving the room.

When the door shut, Aurora exhaled deeply. She looked down at the clothes вҖ” simple denim shorts and a white tank top. At least they were comfortable. She slipped them on, tied her hair into a loose ponytail, and slid her feet into a pair of flip-flops left near the bed.

Taking a steadying breath, she stepped out and followed the faint scent of food down the long corridor.

The dining hall was nothing short of dazzling вҖ” marble floors, a chandelier glittering like stars, and a long table fit for royalty. AuroraвҖҷs eyes darted around nervously until they landed on Nanny Celia, who smiled at her approach.

вҖңIf youвҖҷre searching for your husband,вҖқ Celia said knowingly, вҖңyou wonвҖҷt find him here.вҖқ

Aurora blinked. вҖңHeвҖҷsвҖҰ not joining us?вҖқ

Celia shook her head, her expression softening. вҖңHe rarely eats. Just coffee in the morning and maybe a cookie. ThatвҖҷs all until the next day.вҖқ

AuroraвҖҷs brows furrowed. вҖңThatвҖҷsвҖҰ all he eats?вҖқ she asked, utterly baffled.

вҖңYes,вҖқ Celia said simply.

AuroraвҖҷs lips parted in disbelief. And yet he looks like that? she thought, remembering his broad shoulders and well-toned chest.

вҖңOh,вҖқ she managed quietly.

вҖңCome, dear. Sit down and eat,вҖқ Nanny Celia urged.

Aurora glanced at the grand chair set before her. Something about it felt wrong вҖ” too stiff, too formal. вҖңUhmвҖҰ can I maybeвҖҰ sit on the ground instead?вҖқ

Celia nearly choked on her drink. вҖңWhat did you just say?вҖқ

Aurora bit her lip. вҖңI just feel more comfortable that way,вҖқ she murmured.

вҖңDo you sit on the ground at home?вҖқ Celia asked, eyes narrowing.

Aurora hesitated. Her instinct was to nod, but pride made her shake her head instead. вҖңNoвҖҰ not exactly. ItвҖҷs justвҖ”different.вҖқ

вҖңWell, things are different now,вҖқ Nanny Celia said firmly, her tone kind but authoritative. вҖңYouвҖҷre Mrs. Roche. A billionaireвҖҷs wife. You should start acting like one.вҖқ

The reminder sent a strange chill through AuroraвҖҷs stomach. Mrs. Roche. The name still felt foreign, like a costume she wasnвҖҷt ready to wear.

She finally sat down at the dining table, though the chair felt too grand, the cutlery too heavy.

Her eyes widened at the sight before her вҖ” golden-roasted chicken, delicate salads, fluffy rice, glossy desserts lined like jewels. She hadnвҖҷt seen this much food sinceвҖҰ ever.

She picked up the fork and knife hesitantly, then decided formality could wait. She reached for the chicken first, tore off a piece, and took a bite.

Warm, buttery flavor melted on her tongue.

For the first time since stepping into Roche Empire, Aurora smiled вҖ” small, but real.

JASPER MANSION вҳ…

Blood trickled slowly down JasperвҖҷs temple as he stepped into his mansion. The marble floor gleamed beneath his shoes, and the faint scent of expensive cologne clung to the air.

The moment the maids saw him, their faces filled with alarm.

вҖңSirвҖ”your headвҖ”вҖқ one of them gasped, rushing forward.

вҖңNo need,вҖқ Jasper muttered coolly, brushing past them with a dismissive wave. His tone left no room for argument.

He climbed the sweeping staircase, every step echoing faintly through the silent halls. Dennis had already dropped him off and driven homeвҖ”it was far too late for company anyway.

In his room, Jasper tossed his car keys onto the table, strode to his cabinet, and pulled out a first-aid kit. Sitting on the edge of the bed, he opened it, peeled off a bandage, and began tending to his wound.

The sting of disinfectant made him wince. He let out a low groan, then laughed softly to himself.

вҖңPlaying hard to get, are we?вҖқ he murmured, picturing AdrianaвҖҷs furious eyes and the way sheвҖҷd smashed that bottle without hesitation.

The smirk that curved his lips was equal parts amusement and challenge.

He stripped off his blood-stained shirt and tossed it carelessly onto the floor before heading into the bathroom. The shower roared to life, steam filling the space as water cascaded down his toned body, washing away the traces of the night.

Minutes later, he stepped back into his room, towel draped around his neck, hair damp and tousled.

He grabbed his laptop, opened it, and logged into his social media account. The first thing that greeted him made him freeze.

A video.

AdrianaвҖҷs video.

The exact moment the bottle shattered against his headвҖ”looped, shared, and captioned by thousands.

#BillionaireGetsOwned
#SweetSweetClubChaos

He exhaled sharply and shut the laptop with a snap.

Of course it had gone viral. In his world, even a bruise could make headlines.

вҖңFГ»ck,вҖқ he muttered under his breath, running a hand through his hair.

Then, despite everything, a crooked smile ghosted across his lips.

вҖңSheвҖҷs got fire,вҖқ he said softly, a flicker of admiration in his voice. вҖңAnd now sheвҖҷs on my radar.вҖқ

MOREAU RESIDENCE вҳ…

The evening news played softly on the TV, the reporterвҖҷs voice echoing through the quiet living room. Jason sat forward, elbows resting on his knees, lost in the headlinesвҖ”until the sound of sharp heels clicking against the stairs broke the silence.

Margret descended gracefully, her expression unreadable until she reached for the remote and abruptly turned off the TV.

Jason frowned. вҖңWhy would you do that? I was watching something important.вҖқ

Margret mimicked his tone mockingly before her face hardened. вҖңYou should be in the kitchen by now, Jason. ItвҖҷs dinner time, and since AuroraвҖҷs gone, youвҖҷll be cooking for the both of us.вҖқ

Jason blinked, disbelief flashing across his face. вҖңWhat the hell are you talking about?вҖқ

вҖңI said start cooking!вҖқ she snapped, voice slicing through the air like a blade.

Jason rose to his feet. вҖңYouвҖҷre the woman of this house, Margret. You should be making dinner, not me.вҖқ

Her lips curled into a bitter smile. вҖңWoman of the house?вҖқ she repeated coldly. вҖңDo you remember who I was before you dragged me into this miserable life? I was an heiress, Jason! I left everythingвҖ”money, family, statusвҖ”all for you. I was disowned because I was foolish enough to fall for a man who had nothing to offer. So yes, youвҖҷll do whatever I say.вҖқ

JasonвҖҷs voice trembled with anger and hurt. вҖңYou did that because you loved me, not because I forced you.вҖқ

MargretвҖҷs gaze burned with resentment. вҖңI did it because I thought I loved you. But I was wrong. You and that girlвҖ”вҖқ her voice dripped venom, вҖңвҖ”you ruined everything. I shouldвҖҷve never given birth to her. SheвҖҷs the reason my life fell apart.вҖқ

Jason froze. The words cut deeper than she knew. He turned away, blinking back the tears that threatened to spill. Without another word, he walked toward the kitchen.

As he turned on the stove, memories flooded in. He could still see that nightвҖ”years agoвҖ”when Margret went into labor and they had nothing. HeвҖҷd run through the rain to Gabriel Roche, desperate, begging for help. Gabriel had agreed on one condition: that one day, JasonвҖҷs daughter would marry his son. Jason had been too desperate to refuse.

He still remembered how Margret wanted to abandon Aurora after giving birth. HeвҖҷd pleaded with her, explaining the deal, and that was the only reason she kept the child.

Now, all that hatred had festered into something uglier.

Jason sighed deeply, wiping his face with the back of his hand as the flame on the gas cooker flickered to life.

He had loved Margret enough to endure her cruelty. But hearing her say she no longer loved himвҖ”that she regretted their daughterвҖҷs existenceвҖ”made something inside him break quietly.

The spoon trembled in his hand as he stirred the pot.

вҖңMaybeвҖҰвҖқ he whispered under his breath, voice cracking, вҖңвҖҰmaybe love was never enough.вҖқ

ROCHE MANSION вңҰ

McKenzie adjusted the cuffs of his dark shirt as he stepped out of his fatherвҖҷs room. His expression was unreadable, cold as usual, yet a faint hint of exhaustion traced the edges of his eyes. The hallway lights glowed dimly, washing everything in soft gold.

He was halfway down the hall when a small figure nearly collided with him. Aurora.

Her damp hair clung to her neck, and her fingers fidgeted nervously as she quickly lowered her head. вҖңIвҖ“IвҖҷm sorry,вҖқ she murmured, her voice trembling just enough for him to hear.

McKenzieвҖҷs lips twitched in faint amusement. She still apologizes for everything.

вҖңIвҖҰ I lost my way,вҖқ she added quickly, keeping her eyes glued to the floor.

He tilted his head slightly, studying her. вҖңLost your way?вҖқ he repeated slowly, as though testing the sound of her words. вҖңThis house isnвҖҷt that big, Mrs. Roche.вҖқ

She swallowed hard at the title. вҖңIвҖ“IвҖҷll be careful next time.вҖқ

вҖңLift your head,вҖқ he ordered suddenly. His voice wasnвҖҷt loud, but it carried the kind of command that made her obey before she thought twice.

Her lashes fluttered as she finally looked up, and he caught the nervous flicker in her eyes. She looked fragileвҖ”too fragile for someone now tied to his name.

вҖңAbout the wedding nightвҖҰвҖқ he began, watching how her posture stiffened instantly.

AuroraвҖҷs breath hitched. вҖңIвҖҷm notвҖҰ ready yet,вҖқ she said softly, her words tumbling over each other. вҖңI know I should be, but IвҖҷm not. I mean, youвҖҷre my husband and all, but I justвҖ”вҖқ

McKenzie chuckled quietly, cutting her off. вҖңRelax. IвҖҷm not interested.вҖқ

Her brows furrowed. вҖңWhвҖ“what?вҖқ

вҖңThis marriage isnвҖҷt about love or desire,вҖқ he said flatly. вҖңItвҖҷs a contract, remember? So you can calm your nerves. YouвҖҷre not my type anyway.вҖқ

The words landed sharper than he intended, and he noticed the faint glimmer of tears in her eyes. Still, he didnвҖҷt take them back.

вҖңYвҖ“yeah,вҖқ she whispered, forcing a shaky smile. вҖңA loveless marriage. No feelings attached.вҖқ

вҖңGood.вҖқ He nodded curtly and then pointed toward the door behind him. вҖңOne more thingвҖ”youвҖҷre not allowed in there. ThatвҖҷs my fatherвҖҷs room. DonвҖҷt ever step foot near it.вҖқ

He walked past her without another glance, his scentвҖ”clean, crisp, expensiveвҖ”lingering in the air long after he was gone.

Aurora stood frozen for a moment before turning toward the hallway again. Her gaze lingered on the forbidden door. Why canвҖҷt I go in there? she thought. But she quickly dismissed it and walked away, her slippers soft against the marble floor.

Back in her room, she sank onto the bed. The mattress was soft, far too comfortable for someone like her, and yet she felt no peace. She reached for her cracked phoneвҖ”the same one sheвҖҷd been using for yearsвҖ”and unlocked it.

Nora and AlvinвҖҷs names flashed across her screen. Dozens of missed messages. Her heart squeezed with guilt.

She pressed the video call button, hoping theyвҖҷd pick up.

вҖңFinally!вҖқ NoraвҖҷs face appeared first, eyes wide. вҖңAurora, where have you been? We thought you got kidnapped or something!вҖқ

Aurora laughed awkwardly. вҖңNo, IвҖҷm fine. I justвҖҰ got busy.вҖқ

вҖңBusy?вҖқ Alvin chimed in, looking skeptical. вҖңSo busy that you ignored us for two days straight?вҖқ

вҖңI told you, my mom took my phone. She didnвҖҷt let me go to college today,вҖқ Aurora lied smoothly, her voice light but her chest tight.

вҖңUgh, sheвҖҷs always like that,вҖқ Nora sighed. вҖңYou need to move out someday.вҖқ

Aurora smiled faintly. If only you knew.

They chatted about random thingsвҖ”their classes, gossip, a new showвҖ”but AuroraвҖҷs mind drifted. The vast mansion was silent except for the low hum of night. She felt small, trapped in a world that didnвҖҷt belong to her.

When the call finally ended, she set her phone down and lay back. The ceiling stared down at her, elegant and empty.

A soft breeze brushed through the open window. Somewhere far off in the house, a door clicked shut.

Aurora closed her eyes, wishingвҖ”just for a secondвҖ”that probably if she had a better life, lovely and caring parents but anyways it's life after all for her.

ROCHE MANSION вңҰ NEXT MORNING

Soft rays of sunlight streamed through the tall, cream-colored curtains, warming the room in a golden haze. Aurora stirred beneath the thick duvet, her lashes fluttering before she buried herself deeper into the sheets. For a moment, she panickedвҖ”her motherвҖҷs angry voice echoing faintly in her half-dreaming mindвҖ”but then it hit her.

She wasnвҖҷt at home.
She was married now.

The thought alone made her chest tighten.

вҖңMy lady,вҖқ a gentle voice called.

AuroraвҖҷs brows furrowed, and she slowly opened her eyes to see an unfamiliar face hovering above herвҖ”a lady dressed neatly in the Roche staff uniform, her smile soft and practiced.

вҖңMy lady,вҖқ the girl repeated, her tone polite but warm.

Aurora blinked a few times, her gaze darting around the large bedroomвҖ”the pale marble floor, the grand mirror, the faint scent of lavender in the air. She wasnвҖҷt dreaming. This was her new reality.

вҖңGood morning, my lady. IвҖҷm glad youвҖҷre awake,вҖқ the girl said cheerfully.

Aurora rubbed her eyes and sat up, her hair a tousled mess. вҖңUmвҖҰ why are you calling me my lady?вҖқ she asked, tilting her head. вҖңYou look older than me. ShouldnвҖҷt it be the other way around?вҖқ

The girl chuckled softly. вҖңThatвҖҷs not how it works here. YouвҖҷre married to my master, so youвҖҷll be addressed properly.вҖқ

Aurora blinked at her for a moment before giving a faint smile. вҖңOhвҖҰ but just call me Rora. IвҖҷm not used to all this вҖҳmy ladyвҖҷ talk. My nameвҖҷs AuroraвҖ”and you are?вҖқ

вҖңMira,вҖқ the girl replied, smiling again.

Aurora laughed quietly. вҖңYou really love smiling, donвҖҷt you?вҖқ

Mira only grinned wider, adjusting the tray in her hands. вҖңMaster said I should help you get dressed. YouвҖҷre both going somewhere today.вҖқ

AuroraвҖҷs eyes widened. вҖңWe are?вҖқ

вҖңYes, maвҖҷam.вҖқ

вҖңOhвҖҰвҖқ She hesitated, then added quickly, вҖңBut shouldnвҖҷt I have breakfast first?вҖқ

вҖңOf course,вҖқ Mira said, setting the tray gently on the bedside table. The aroma of buttered toast, eggs, and hot tea filled the room.

Aurora nodded, sliding out of bed. вҖңRight. Breakfast first.вҖқ

Mira followed her toward the bathroom door. Aurora glanced once more at her reflection in the mirrorвҖ”barefaced, sleepy, uncertainвҖ”and sighed.

вҖңSomehow,вҖқ she muttered to herself, вҖңthis doesnвҖҷt feel real.вҖқ

And with that, she disappeared into the bathroom, the sound of running water mixing with the soft hum of morning inside Roche Mansion.

DOWNSTAIRS вҳ…

Aurora took a deep breath before descending the grand staircase. The morning light streamed through the chandelier, scattering golden reflections across the marble floor. Her soft slippers made no sound as she walked down, clutching the railing for balance.

At the dining table sat McKenzie вҖ” elegant in a charcoal suit, hair neatly styled, a porcelain cup of coffee in his hand. Just as Nanny Celia had said, there was a small plate beside him with a single cookie.

вҖңGood morning,вҖқ Aurora greeted politely, her voice gentle but unsure.

McKenzie didnвҖҷt lift his eyes from the newspaper he was scanning. He simply sipped his coffee as if she were invisible.

Aurora blinked, then scoffed quietly and sat at the far end of the table. How rude, she thought, crossing her arms.

Her eyes, however, betrayed her annoyance вҖ” she couldnвҖҷt help but sneak glances at him. The way he held his cup, the way the sunlight traced the sharp edges of his faceвҖҰ he looked effortlessly composed.

Without looking up, McKenzie muttered, вҖңStealing glances, huh?вҖқ

Aurora choked on her own spit, eyes wide. вҖңN-no, I wasnвҖҷt!вҖқ she stammered, coughing softly.

A faint smirk tugged at his lips, though he hid it behind his cup. He stood up, straightened his suit, and said coolly, вҖңMeet me outside.вҖқ

Before she could ask why, he was already walking out.

The executives exchanged approving glances, jotting notes as she spoke. A few nodded along, clearly impressed.

Kenzie finally stood, closing his laptop. вҖңExcellent. This project stays top priority for the next quarter. I want regular updates вҖ” and results.вҖқ

A few executives stood and shook his hand, congratulating Monica on a solid presentation before filing out of the room. The sound of polite chatter and shuffling papers filled the air as everyone began to leave.

Monica hurried after Kenzie, holding her tablet. вҖңSir, you have a meeting with Mr. King at noon.вҖқ

вҖңCancel it,вҖқ Kenzie replied flatly, already halfway down the hall.

вҖңSirвҖ”вҖқ

He stopped and turned, eyes sharp enough to make her flinch. вҖңI said cancel it.вҖқ

вҖңYвҖ“yes, sir,вҖқ Monica stammered, lowering her head as he walked away.

Once he was gone, she exhaled and smiled faintly to herself.
вҖңSooner or later,вҖқ she whispered under her breath, вҖңheвҖҷll be mine.вҖқ

SSC COLLEGE

Aurora stepped out of the sleek black car, waving briefly at Ruby and Abby before they drove off. 

Every eye on the compound seemed to turn her way. Her black halter top paired perfectly with the pink denim skirt that hugged her curves, and the black heels clicked softly on the pavement. SheвҖҷd straightened her hair that morning and tied a cute ribbon at the end, giving her that mix of elegance and edge.

As she walked toward her friends, the whispers began вҖ” quiet, curious, admiring.

вҖңGood morning,вҖқ Aurora greeted cheerfully, her voice cutting through the murmurs.

Nora smiled faintly and replied, вҖңMorning.вҖқ Alvin, however, stayed silent.

Aurora frowned slightly. вҖңWhatвҖҷs wrong, Alvin?вҖқ

They were usually close вҖ” always joking, always talking вҖ” but today his quietness stood out. Nora shifted awkwardly beside them, suddenly feeling like a third wheel.

вҖңI messaged you last night,вҖқ Alvin muttered, looking away. вҖңYou didnвҖҷt reply. I waited all night thinking youвҖҷd text me back.вҖқ

Aurora blinked, realizing she had completely forgotten after Mira had barged into her room and dragged her into cooking duty.

вҖңOh, that!вҖқ she gasped softly. вҖңIвҖҷm really sorry. I totally forgot.вҖқ

Alvin tried to stay annoyed, but one look at her apologetic face and his expression melted. вҖңYouвҖҷre forgiven,вҖқ he teased, вҖңbut only if you buy me lunch today.вҖқ

Aurora grinned. вҖңDeal.вҖқ

They both laughed, and then her gaze shifted to Nora, who looked paler than usual.

вҖңHey, you okay?вҖқ Aurora asked gently.

вҖңSheвҖҷs having stomach cramps,вҖқ Alvin explained before Nora could speak.

Aurora frowned in concern. вҖңYou shouldвҖҷve stayed home.вҖқ

вҖңIвҖҷll be fine,вҖқ Nora said weakly with a small smile.

Just then, a group of students hurried past them toward the main building. Alvin checked his watch. вҖңWe should go. Professor Harris doesnвҖҷt tolerate latecomers.вҖқ

Aurora nodded, adjusting her bag on her shoulder. вҖңLetвҖҷs move then.вҖқ

 CLASSROOMвҳ…

The lecturer was already in class when Aurora, Alvin, and Nora walked in. Students quickly scrambled to their seats as his sharp voice filled the room.

вҖңEveryone, bring out your assignments. IвҖҷll be collecting them right now.вҖқ

Aurora smiled, reaching into her bag and pulling out her neatly stapled pages вҖ” the same one Kenzie had helped her finish just last night. She passed it to Alvin, who, as class minister, went around collecting everyoneвҖҷs work.

вҖңDid you finish yours?вҖқ Aurora whispered to Nora.

вҖңYeah, before the cramps hit,вҖқ Nora replied weakly.

Aurora nodded and turned to Alvin, who was already holding a tall stack of papers.

вҖңDonвҖҷt forget mine,вҖқ she teased.

вҖңI wouldnвҖҷt dare,вҖқ Alvin smirked, taking hers and heading to the lecturerвҖҷs desk.

The lecturer glanced through the pile. вҖңGood. IвҖҷll grade these before tomorrowвҖҷs class. Some of you better pray you didnвҖҷt copy from last semesterвҖҷs notes again.вҖқ

A few students laughed nervously. Aurora stayed silent, feeling a flicker of pride вҖ” and guilt.

As the lecture continued, she could feel eyes on her. Stacy and Michelle, seated a few rows ahead, kept turning around to glare at her. Aurora rolled her eyes.

Jealous much? she thought.

When class finally ended, the lecturer reminded them, вҖңIвҖҷll return your graded assignments next class. Dismissed.вҖқ

Students began packing up. Alvin slung his bag over his shoulder and leaned toward Aurora.

вҖңYou still owe me lunch, remember?вҖқ

Aurora chuckled. вҖңI know, I know. IвҖҷll treat you today.вҖқ

вҖңIвҖҷm coming too,вҖқ Nora added, standing up slowly.

вҖңOf course you are,вҖқ Aurora smiled as the trio left the hall.

JASPER CONGLOMERATE вҖ“ CEOвҖҷS OFFICE

JasperвҖҷs grandmother leaned back in her chair with a satisfied smile after the interview. She had asked Adriana a dozen difficult questions вҖ” and the young woman had answered every one with confidence and grace.

вҖңIвҖҷm proud of you, son,вҖқ his grandmother said, turning to Jasper. вҖңYou did well in choosing the right person.вҖқ

Jasper flashed a grin, his perfect white teeth glinting under the office lights.
вҖңWhy then am I my fatherвҖҷs son?вҖқ he quipped, and she chuckled softly.

вҖңI should be going now,вҖқ she said, rising from her seat.

вҖңIвҖҷll tell the driver to drop you off. Bye, Granny,вҖқ Jasper replied, waving politely as she exited the office.

The moment the door clicked shut, his charming smile vanished. His expression turned sharp and cold.

вҖңWhatвҖҷs my schedule for today?вҖқ he asked in a low, commanding tone.

Adriana quickly opened the file she held and began reading out the dayвҖҷs itinerary. Her voice trembled slightly, but she kept going until she reached the end.

вҖңI didnвҖҷt hear any of that,вҖқ Jasper interrupted, leaning back in his chair. вҖңMind reading it again?вҖқ

Adriana frowned, snapping the file shut and placing it on the desk.
вҖңIвҖҷm not reading anything again,вҖқ she said firmly.

A dangerous smirk tugged at JasperвҖҷs lips.
вҖңYou know, my grandmother was wrong to praise you. You donвҖҷt deserve it.вҖқ

He stood up, his gaze hard and unflinching.
вҖңMaybe youвҖҷve forgotten that I own every part of you now.вҖқ

Adriana froze. вҖңWhat do you mean?вҖқ

Jasper reached into a drawer and pulled out a document вҖ” the one she had signed. He placed it in front of her, tapping it once with his finger.

вҖңRead it again.вҖқ

AdrianaвҖҷs eyes darted over the paper. Her voice shook as she read the words aloud:

Rule No. 1: You must be submissive to me.
Rule No. 2: I have full dominance over you.
Rule No. 3: I am your superior.

She looked up at him in disbelief. вҖңYou must be joking.вҖқ

Jasper chuckled darkly, spinning the dice in his hand as he leaned against his desk.
вҖңDoes it look like IвҖҷm joking? You signed those terms yourself вҖ” no one forced you. That means you do exactly as I say. If I want you in my house, youвҖҷll come. If I want you in my bed, youвҖҷll be there. My needs come first вҖ” always.вҖқ

AdrianaвҖҷs throat tightened. вҖңIвҖҷm not giving in to this. Never.вҖқ

вҖңThen get ready to pay one hundred million dollars,вҖқ Jasper said smoothly. вҖңWe both know you donвҖҷt have that kind of money. So, youвҖҷll work for me вҖ” for five years вҖ” until you do.вҖқ

Tears welled up in AdrianaвҖҷs eyes. вҖңI know why youвҖҷre doing this. If itвҖҷs because I hit you with a bottle and kicked you in the groin, then IвҖҷm sorry!вҖқ

вҖңIf youвҖҷd accepted earlier,вҖқ he replied coolly, вҖңnone of this would have happened. But nowвҖҰвҖқ he leaned closer, his voice dangerously calm, вҖңyouвҖҷre mine. My secretary. My cook. My rules.вҖқ

вҖңYou canвҖҷt do that!вҖқ she shouted, her voice breaking. вҖңI have a son to take care of!вҖқ

But Jasper was already walking out the door. He didnвҖҷt even look back.

CAFETERIA вҖ” SSC COLLEGE

The cafeteria buzzed with noise as students stood in a long queue, trays in hand, waiting for lunch. Aurora joined the line, smiling faintly as she scrolled through her phone. Just when it was almost her turn, Stacy appeared out of nowhere, snatched her tray, and boldly walked to the front.

AuroraвҖҷs eyes widened. вҖңWaitвҖ”what do you think youвҖҷre doing?вҖқ

Stacy turned with a mocking smirk. вҖңYou came late, and you still think you can stand in line like everyone else? Who do you think you are?вҖқ

Aurora crossed her arms. вҖңI think IвҖҷm someone who knows how to wait her turn. Unlike you.вҖқ

A few students nearby gasped softly. StacyвҖҷs smirk darkened.
вҖңWowвҖҰ look who suddenly grew a spine. Because you showed up with a new look, right? You think that transformation makes you better than us?вҖқ

Aurora clenched her jaw, trying to keep calm, but Stacy wasnвҖҷt done. She raised her voice so everyone could hear.

вҖңHave any of you wondered how Aurora suddenly showed up with a brand-new phone and fancy clothes? Or how sheвҖ”the girl who always failedвҖ”suddenly got the highest mark in the last assignment?вҖқ

The cafeteria grew quiet. Heads turned. Phones lifted, cameras ready.

вҖңDonвҖҷt call me a failure,вҖқ Aurora shot back, her voice trembling. вҖңI justвҖҰ didnвҖҷt have time to study before. My momвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңOh please!вҖқ Stacy cut in, laughing coldly. вҖңWe all know the truth. YouвҖҷve been sleeping with men for money. ThatвҖҷs how you afforded everything, right?вҖқ

The crowd gasped.

вҖңSay it, Aurora! Deny it if you can!вҖқ Stacy sneered.

AuroraвҖҷs lips parted, but no words came out. Her throat tightened, her hands shaking. Stacy smiled in triumph вҖ” her plan was working perfectly.

Then, from behind the crowd, a calm yet firm voice broke through the whispers.

вҖңSheвҖҷs definitely not into prostitution.вҖқ

Everyone turned toward the sound вҖ” the confident footsteps growing louder as the crowd parted slowlyвҖҰ

                 (вҖўрқҗғрқҗ”рқҗҢрқҗҢрқҗҳ;рқҗҲ рқҗӢрқҗҺрқҗ•рқҗ„ рқҗҳрқҗҺрқҗ”вҖў)

!!Yes... She knew she didn't deserve Newton after everything that happened five years ago on their 18th birthday.

It kept replaying in her head, and she picked up the pen to sign the divorce papers..... 

She signed the papers, and Jarvis smiled, collecting the papers from her.

"Thank you for signing the papers," Jarvis said with a crooked voice.

"I did what you asked me to do," Joanna said.

"Not all, I still have more requests," Jarvis said.

"And what's that?" Joanna asked

"You should leave the house you're no longer married to Newton, so leave," Jarvis muttered with a smirk.

"You're not serious, are you?" Joanna asked

"Does it look like I'm joking? You have to leave because your service here is no longer needed, you see." Jarvis said.

"I can't do that, I will wait to inform Newton. He needs to give me his permission before I leave, I'm serious," Joanna said.

"I guess you want it the hard way," Jarvis muttered, taking her hand before dragging her out.

Then she locked the door. She went upstairs and put all of Joanna's belongings into her luggage, then went downstairs and threw the luggage outside, then locked the door.

While Joanna kept banging the door, nevertheless, Jarvis didn't hear her.

Joanna cried for a while before she wiped her tears. She knew she had made a mistake by signing the papers, but she felt guilty for what had happened to Jarvis five years ago.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

Jarvis sat on the chair with a bright smile on her face. She was just too happy.

Joanna signing the divorce papers and her being with Newton again everything was just so overwhelming.

She loved the way her sister would slowly abide by her will and she had been using what had happened five years ago against Joanna.

"My sister is sure crazy, a fool," she laughed murderously and stood up.

Then she walked to the kitchen, where she saw Alison making breakfast.

"What are you making?" she asked, and Alison looked at her. Her tone, everything, was just so different she immediately knew that it was Jarvis.

"Joanna said I should prepare mashed potatoes," Alison replied.

"I don't want that you can just prepare macaroni," Jarvis said.

"But I'm already preparing it," Alison said.

"Are you arguing with me already, huh?As you can see, the real owner of the house is back, so you will simply do what I asked you to do," Jarvis fired at her.

"My boss is not yet married to you, so I'll prepare the mashed potatoes," Alison said, but a slap landed on her cheek.

Jarvis walked to her and grabbed her hair... Alison cried in pain... Jarvis just smirked.

"Do you think I'm Joanna? Oh no! I'm Jarvis I'm different from my sister."Jarvis said, as she grabbed her hair harder.

Yeah, this woman here was so different from Joanna... She's a monster.

"Are you going to prepare the macaroni for me, or do you want it the hard way?" Jarvis asked.

"I'll prepare it," Alison quickly said.

"Good girl," Jarvis said and released her.

"If what I did gets to your boss, not only will I remove every strand of your hair, but I will also break your teeth," Jarvis muttered.

"Sure, I won't inform my boss," Alison muttered, and Jarvis smirked.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

BURIAL GROUND**

Mr. Fallon's body had already been buried, and Jimmy and Angel turned to leave when the reporters swarmed them.

*Sir, we have seen this girl with you on different occasions! 

*We just want to know who she is to you!

*You have always been single, so we wouldn't be surprised if she's your girlfriend!

*We just need you to say the word.

Jimmy looked at Angel, who was standing like a statue. This was the right moment for him to introduce her to the media, and then his friends would follow suit.

He grabbed her and gently placed his hand on her waist. Angel looked at him.

*Sir, we need an answer!

"You are all suspiciously right, but she's not my girlfriend, but my wife," Jimmy said, and Angel looked at him. He smiled back at her.

*What?

*When did you guys tie the knot?

*Was it after Mr. Fallon's death or before his death?"

"Actually, it was before my dad died, and he was aware of it... This girl here is my life... My woman, and I'll sacrifice the whole world for her," Jimmy said, and Angel didn't know when she smiled.

*Awwww!

*She's so lucky!

*Your wife is beautiful!

*But sir, we would want a real wedding!

"Sure, we will get married again, so I'll let you guys know when the date is actually fixed," Jimmy muttered.

*Thanks!

*Sir, just a kiss, please!

Angel's eyes widened when she heard that, and Jimmy moved his lips to her ear.

"Wifey, there is nothing bad in just a kiss," he muttered, then he cupped her face in his hand and placed a light yet tender kiss on her lips.

*At this rate, I think I'm going to faint.

*This is so amazing!

"If you wish, can we leave?" Jimmy asked.

*Sure!"

He took Angel's hand, and they walked and got into their car. Angel faced him.

"What was that you pulled over there? Do you know the video will go so viral?" Angel said.

"And I don't care I want the whole world to know that you're mine, that you're my woman, and don't tell me you didn't enjoy what I did out there," Jimmy asked.

Angel looked away from him and smiled.

"You see, you love it," Jimmy said.

"Stop dreaming," Angel said, and Jimmy chuckled.

In a secluded area, Mikel's phone popped, and the video of how Angel and Jimmy kissed showed on his phone.

Since she left... No, since he broke up with her, his life had been miserable. He regretted ever leaving her.

He cursed his mother for giving him a bad idea to let go of the woman who was ready to sacrifice her life for him.

He really lost a gem.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

SвҖўKвҖўJ HIGH SCHOOL**

The principal walked inside the hall and stood beside the pulpit, immediately the students saw her they kept quiet.

"Hello Everyone," the principal said, and the students just smiled.

"So as you all know, the year semester is coming to an end, and the seniors are graduating." The principal paused, then continued.

"This will be the first time we are going to be hosting our prom night with both the juniors and the seniors." The principal said, and the students murmured in excitement.

*Awnnn!!

*That's nice!!"

"We would only do this prom night if you all pass your exams excellently, so we would love you all to prepare for the exams, then the prom night. Remember, you all have to choose your partner, because there will only be one prom queen and a king." The principal said again.

"And good luck to whoever will be winning this year's king and queen." She said, and the students clapped.

*It's sure me and my partner!!

*No, it's I and my partner!! 

*Who can beat against me and my partner!!"

Piper just rolled her eyes on hearing that. The prom night hadn't even come, and they were already arguing about who was going to win. She saw Kiss and walked up to him.

"Hi." She said, and Kiss smiled before giving her a kiss on the cheek.

"The prom night, will you be participating?" Piper asked.

"I'll only participate if you score an A."Kiss said.

"What." Piper widened her eyes.

"Yeah.. Score an A, and you'll be my prom queen." Kiss said.

"Sure.. I'll try my best.. I'm studying already I'm always paying attention to the lectures." Piper said.

"I trust my girl."Kiss said.

"But you don't have to be rude what if I don't score an A?" Piper asked.

"I know my cutie doll  you will score an A." Kiss said.

"Do you trust me that much? Huh?" Piper asked.

"Yeah." Kiss replied, and Piper smiled. Then Kiss pulled her closer, inhaling her candy scent.

"I love your perfume." Kiss said.

"Is it the perfume you love, or my body?" Piper asked.

"Both." He replied.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

MOORE GROUP OF COMPANY**
 
Newton came out of the conference room, same as Leo. He picked up his phone and dialed Joanna's number, but it wasn't going through.

"Why are you feeling nervous?" Leo asked.

"Joanna isn't picking up my call," Newton replied, 

"She might be angry with you," Leo said with a wink.

"Come on, I didn't offend her at all, I'm not sure I did something wrong to her?" Newton said.

"Let me try calling her." Leo said. He also dialed Joanna's number, but it wouldn't go through.

"Did she pick up?" Newton asked.

"Same thing, it's not going through." Leo said.

"I guess I'll just have to go home. It's almost closing hour."Newton muttered. 

"Sure." Leo replied.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

Newton came out from his car and rushed inside his mansion.

"Joanna." Newton called, but Jarvis stepped down the stairs.

"You're back," Jarvis asked, and Newton rolled his eyes. He recognized her immediately.

"Where's Joanna?" Newton asked.

"She left already," Jarvis said.

"What do you mean by that?" Newton asked.

"She said she's tired of this marriage, so that's why she left. I know you might think I'm lying, but check that out," Jarvis said, handling over the divorce papers to him.

Newton scrolled through the divorce papers and shook his head.

"It's not my Joanna... No, it can't be," Newton said, and Jarvis smirked.

"That's the fact, Newton... She left and signed the divorce papers... She claims you weren't treating her well, and she was tired of this hellish marriage," Jarvis said, and it was like she unlocked the beast in Newton, as he grabbed her and started choking her.

"I wouldn't repeat this again... Where's my wife?" Newton asked, choking her, and Jarvis could swear that if he should release her, there would surely be a mark on her skin.

"I swear to you, I don't know where she is... She just took her luggage and left," Jarvis said.

"She didn't leave on her own, I know you said something that made her leave... Now tell me, what did you say to her?" Newton asked with a cold voice.

"I just reminded her of an incident that occurred five years ago, that's all," Jarvis coughed as Newton released her.

He rushed out of the mansion immediately, and the rain started falling dangerously.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

Joanna squatted on the floor with her luggage beside her, she was shivering as the rain hit her hard.

Her tears were invisible because of the rain.

She hates this life... Just when she thought she found her happiness, someone with whom she could collide, all this had to happen.

She hated herself for everything that had happened five years ago, but she hated herself more for signing the divorce papers.

She suddenly looked up and saw Newton standing beside her... Was she hallucinating? No, she wasn't, because he was really standing beside her.

She sprang up immediately, and Newton made to take a step towards her, but she stopped him.

"Stay away from me, Newton you'd probably hate me if you find out about what happened five years ago," Joanna said.

"I don't care," Newton said and took a step.

"I'm serious... Stay away from me," Joanna said, but he ignored her and ran to hug her.

Just the warmth of his embrace made her think about what had happened five years ago.

FLASHBACK BACKвҖў FIVE YEARS AGO**

It was their 18th birthday, and Joanna rushed inside the room to see her sister, Jarvis, sleeping soundly on their bed.

She rushed to her and shook her, then Jarvis blinked her eyes open.

"Today is our birthday, and you're sleeping today is supposed to be a memorable day for both of us," Joanna said.

"I'm not interested," Jarvis said, and she was about to sleep again when Joanna tapped her.

"Come on, let's go and make today a memorable day for both of us... We're no longer sweet teens, but we're youth now... It shows we're getting matured," Joanna said.

"Where do you suggest we go?" Jarvis asked.

"My friend recommended a club it's one of the best in town... We can go partying and enjoying our lives. Please, sis," Joanna pleaded.

"Sure," Jarvis said, and Joanna smiled.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

They went to the club and the waiter ended up serving them alcoholic drinks instead of non-alcoholic drinks that they had requested.

They ended up drinking more than expected, in fact they drank more than they shouldn't have.

"Jo.. I'm going to the restroom.. I can no longer hold it." Jarvis said drunkenly.

"Sure.. I'd just wait here." Joanna said and slammed her head on the table while Jarvis went ahead to use the restroom as she had said.

She went to the restroom and used the facilities, and she was about to come outside when she bumped into a man. 

She look up and her cute poppy innocence eyes met with a cold and dangerous face. 

They were two and she made to leave but they hold her hand and pushed her into the restroom then they locked the door. 

"What are you guys doing?." Jarvis tipsy voice said. 

"Shut up and let's do our business. " the two men said and started unbuttoning their shirts.. They ended up raping her. 

END OF FLASHBACK**

"Yeah, they raped my sister. If only I hadn't suggested that we go to the club, then they wouldn't have raped her. I am behind her misfortune. She hated the way their touches felt on her, and then she started sleeping with different men just so she couldn't feel their touches on her. She would have ended up with you, but her crave for sex didn't allow her to stay." Joanna shouted.

"You asked me why I would allow my sister to belittle me, and I said, what would a sister have done? I did all I did for her sake, just for her to be happy. I know you hate me now, and I deserve it. Please just go back to my sister else, she's gonna be hurt. I don't deserve you, Newton." Joanna said.

"You think I'd hate you, and that you don't deserve me? All that happened to Jarvis was because of fate. We're not the ones who write our destiny, but the Almighty did. Our fate has already been written we're meant to be together, and please don't push me away from you..." Newton said as the rain continued to hit them.

"Newton, I don't deserve your love..." Joanna said, and Newton claimed her lips.

"Can't you see I love you, dummy? I love you, and if you keep pushing me away, I might just die..." Newton said and pulled her close, reconnecting their lips, and Joanna wrapped her hands around his neck.

The rain made the kiss hotter.

That hand she could remember it... It was Nelly's hand... She could feel the hand... The chemical Nelly poured on her skin really damaged her, and the swollen boils and peels... It was surely Nelly.

"I didn't see the person, but I'm suspecting someone."Piper muttered. 

"Who?" Kiss asked.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

SAME NIGHT, NELLY APARTMENT**

She entered her house, removed the mask, and threw it on the couch.

She smirked and went to her wardrobe to get the ointment she usually applied to her hand and face. She looked at her hands and scoffed.

"By now, it was supposed to be you in this condition, but I'm the one in it... The whole school thought I was a changed person, including Piper - what a fool! They didn't know I was just biding my time, waiting for the perfect moment to execute my plan... Humans are so gullible." She laughed maniacally. 

"Piper, I did say that if I couldn't have Kiss, no one would. But you didn't listen you chose to accept him as your boyfriend. But unfortunately, he can't be your boyfriend anymore... Rest in peace, dear Piper. Now, let's see how you're going to attend prom night with him. Kiss will have no choice but to ask me to be his prom queen... Nelly, ain't I good?" she said, about to apply the ointment when someone grabbed it and splashed it on her face.

Then, a welcoming slap landed on her - three consecutive ones. She looked up to see Kiss standing in front of her.

"And you think trying to hurt her would make me run back to you, huh? How can you be this cheap and heartless, wanting to take your fellow human's life?" Kiss asked.

"I don't know what you're saying," Nelly shouted.

"Oh, cut that crap," he snapped.

"You tried to hurt Piper you stabbed her, and you aren't even feeling any remorse. People like you deserve to be in jail." Kiss shouted. 

"Yeah, I don't feel remorse. I used to be Piper's friend, but she stole you away from me." Nelly shouted back at him.

"How? Remember, I was never yours to begin with." Kiss said.

"I used to love you, but you wouldn't even notice me all you see is Piper, Piper, and Piper, and I'm f**king tired. What do you see in her, huh? You turned me into what I am today... I turned into a devil because you didn't want to reciprocate my love. What could I have done? My heart chose you, but..."

"But my heart chose her from the very beginning, Nelly. I was never attracted to you, not even for a single bit. I never loved you." Kiss cut her off.

"Then you can reciprocate my love... Piper is dead already, so you can love me, huh? I promise you'll forget about her." Nelly said, trying to kiss him.

Kiss pushed her away.

"For your fucking information, Piper is not dead." Kiss shouted.

"What?" she asked.

"You heard me right she's not dead, and for what you've done, you're going to be arrested." Kiss said, and the police entered, handcuffed her, and dragged her away.

"No, no, no, Kiss, you can't do this to me you literally can't... I love you so much... So, please." Nelly shouted as the police put her in the van and drove off.

Kiss ruffled his hair, looking relieved that the ordeal was over.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

NEXT DAY, NEWTON APARTMENT**

Leo and Jarvis got down from the car, and Jarvis looked at Leo, who gave her a smile in return.

"Are you sure about this?" Jarvis asked, and Leo nodded.

"I'm sure you can do this, you need to."

"I have never been so nervous before in my entire life, like this," Jarvis muttered, and Leo held her hand.

"Does this relieve you a little bit?" Newton asked, and she nodded.

They walked into the mansion, where they met Alison.

"Is Joanna in?" Leo asked, and Alison nodded, then looked at Jarvis and shrugged. Jarvis walked up to her and held her hand.

"I'm sorry for what I did to you weeks back, can you forgive me?" Jarvis asked, and Alison looked at Leo, who nodded.

"I have forgiven you, I don't joke with Joanna, and you having the same face as Joanna is quite annoying," Alison replied.

"I'm really sorry," Jarvis said, and Alison pulled her into a hug.

"I have forgiven you, I hope we can become friends like I and Joanna."

"Do you want to be my friend?" Jarvis asked.

"That's if you're okay with it," Alison replied, and Jarvis beamed with a smile.

"I'd love to," Jarvis said, and Alison almost gasped.

"Was this Jarvis or a ghost?" she wondered. 

"Really," she asked, and Jarvis nodded.

"Friends then," Alison muttered, and Jarvis smiled.

Leo watched with amusement, clearly written on his face.

Joanna got down from the stairs, only to see Alison and Jarvis hugging each other. She walked closer to them and touched Alison's forehead. 

"Do you have a fever, you and Jarvis, together?" Joanna asked.

"She's now my friend," Alison said, and Joanna almost laughed.

"You're not serious, are you?" Joanna asked

Jarvis, who let go of Alison and walked to Joanna.

"Sis," Jarvis called, and Joanna shrugged.

"Are you here to mock me, that I'm pregnant for the wrong person, and I don't deserve to be pregnant? If yes, then leave."

"Wait, you're pregnant?? " She asked and Joanna nodded. 

Jarvis hugged her, and Jarvis was a bit startled.

"I'm going to be an aunt, a fucking aunt... Fans come and hear this I'm going to be an aunt," Jarvis screamed and kept hugging her.

"Is this Jarvis or someone else?" she wondered.

"Is this really my sister?" Joanna asked, and Jarvis nodded.

"Okay, I came to apologize, not to mock you, I'm sorry for everything, I mean everything single thing I have done to you, I'm sorry for taking advantage of you even when I knew you were never at fault for my rape, I'm deeply sorry," Jarvis said, and Joanna pulled her into a hug.

If only Jarvis knew how much she had been waiting for all this, if only she knew how much she wanted her back, if only she knew how much she had been waiting for her apologies.

"I'm also sorry, Jarvis I'm sorry... I guess I wasn't a good sister... If only I hadn't suggested we go to that club, then nothing would have caused a rift between us I'm also a bad sister, I guess."Joanna said, as tears ran out from her eyes. 

"I should be the one apologizing, but you stole the line again," Jarvis muttered, with tears also in her eyes.

"I'm sorry," Joanna muttered.

"I'm also sorry," Jarvis muttered, and the two sisters yet again hugged each other in a warm embrace, replacing all their lost moments away from each other. They were crying out their eyes.

Leo and Alison just watched the emotional reunion between the two identical sisters.

Newton also entered the mansion he was just returning from work.

"Okay, what's going on here?" Newton asked, cursing Joanna and Jarvis to break the hug.

"Two sisters' reunion," Leo said and climbed on his back.

"Hey, would you get down?" Newton asked.

"Why didn't you tell me that Joanna is pregnant and that you're going to be a father?" Leo asked.

"Who's going to be a father?" Jimmy said, as he stepped into the mansion with Angel. He actually came to introduce Angel to Newton.

"Good twosome," Newton rolled his eyes.

"Who's going to be a father?" Jimmy asked again.

"Who do you expect if not Newton? His wife is pregnant," Leo said, and Joanna just chuckled.

Angel walked to her and pulled her into a hug.

"Congratulations," Angel said with a smile.

"Thanks," Joanna said.

"Why am I seeing two faces?" Angel asked.

"Actually, she's my sister Jarvis," Joanna said, and Angel gasped.

"Wow, identical," she beamed.

"Yeah," Joanna said and placed a soft kiss on Joanna's cheeks

"Okay everyone, I came here because I want to introduce you all to my damsel," Jimmy muttered.

"Who, who stole this dummy heart?" Newton asked, and Jimmy walked towards her and grabbed her waist. Angel blushed.

"She's my pretty damsel, who came into my life unexpectedly," Jimmy said, and Angel smiled.

"Awwn," Joanna and Jarvis said in unison.

"This calls for celebrations," Leo muttered, and everyone walked to sit at the dining table.

"Let's toast to this good news," Leo muttered, and they all clicked their glasses together.

"Cheers."

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

EIGHT MONTH LATER, CITY HOSPITAL*

The sky shook dangerously, the wind blew heavily, trees and everything shook because of the heavy rain that was about to fall.

The clouds clapped together, and the rain started pouring down.

Inside the ward, Joanna was panting heavily, she was in labor, tears filled her eyes as she looked at Newton, who was beside her. He kissed her tears away.

"You're gonna be fine, you can do this," Newton gave her a word of encouragement.

"Please hold my hand," Joanna said, and he held her hand.

"Just know I love you, Joanna. I have always," Newton said, as the doctor asked her to push.

"One, two, three, push!" the doctor screamed, and Joanna pushed. Tears slowly escaped her eyes she was sweating profusely.

"You're getting there, you just have to push one more time, and then we're done," the nurse said. Joanna looked at Newton  her loud panting increased.

"I'm here for you, trust me when I say I cherish every fucking moment with you. And the day I found out you were pregnant, that was the happiest day of my life," Newton said, and she held his hand tighter and pushed.

The baby's head popped out, and the doctor smiled.

"Congratulations, you did it!" the doctor said, and Newton smiled.

The doctor handed the tiny little creature to him, and tears escaped his eyes... They were tears of joy.

Joanna opened her eyes, and the first thing her eyes caught was her baby... a baby boy.

She looked at her family they were three now... Her, Newton, and their baby joy. She prayed that no evil eyes would cast themselves on her family, which she was going to create.

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

рқ‘¬рқ‘·рқ‘°рқ‘ірқ‘¶рқ‘®рқ‘јрқ‘¬*

Years have passed, and Piper and Kiss are now college students.

Kiss is in his final year (400 level) while Piper is in her second year (200l) at Harvard University.

After many years, Newton finally agreed to let Kiss and Piper date, but warned Kiss not to hurt his sister or face the consequences.

Nelly was recently released from jail and is trying to get accepted into college, but it's proving difficult.

Angel and Jimmy relocated to Paris, Angel's dream country, and got married with a beautiful ceremony. They have a four-year-old daughter and are living happily.

Dorothy opened her own boutique and is doing well, having learned to let go of hatred and jealousy.

Leo confessed his love to Jarvis, and they have been dating for three years, preparing for marriage.

"Hey big bro, would you get your @ss over here?" Moanna called as she ran upstairs to his room.

She's Joanna's second child and first daughter. She opened the door to see her brother Nikki reading a novel.

"Oh my, what's the title of the novel?" Moanna jumped on the bed and asked.

"Why, thought you don't like novels, you said it bores you?" Nikki said, and Moanna smacked his head.

"Did you just smack my head? Have you forgotten I'm the senior here?" Nikki asked.

"I know, dummy anyway, Mom sent for us downstairs," Moanna said, and before Nikki knew it, she had run down the stairs, with him following.

"Mom, help me!" Moanna ran and hid behind Joanna's back.

"Mom, did you see that little brat?" Nikki asked Joanna.

"What did she do this time?" Joanna asked.

"Can you believe she smacked my head? She's always acting as if she's the senior  then I need to bring her ego down... I need to make her realize that I'm the senior," Nikki said.

"I would have been the senior, but you stole that from me," Moanna pointed out, sticking out her tongue.

"How?" Nikki asked.

"Because you and nature cheated me," Moanna said.

Joanna stared at both of them, knowing that if she didn't stop them, they would continue arguing like cats and dogs. 

"Okay, enough of your arguments," Joanna said, placing Moanna on top of the dining chair while Nikki sat on it.

"Where's Dad, Mom?" Moanna asked.

"He called to tell me he's going to be late," Joanna muttered, and the two of them sighed. She served them dinner, and they began eating...

р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“р–Ө“

Joanna closed her laptop, having decided to try something new. As a writer, she was now busy plotting the story of her life, and she had chosen to title it "Sweet Pains".

It was a story of pains from the very beginning, and she glanced back to the time Newton found out she wasn't Jarvis and how he treated her badly. But those pains had turned to love in a way she never expected.

The door squeaked, and Newton stepped out of the bathroom, having returned moments earlier.

"Four eyes," he called, and Joanna walked over to him and hugged him.

"You can never get tired of calling me that name, can you?" she asked.

"Never, the name is now a part of me." Newton muttered, 

They walked to the bed, and Joanna lay on top of him.

"Everything that happens between us, it was worth it - being with you, loving you - it was all worth it," Newton muttered.

"Describe us in one word," Joanna said.

"FOREVER," Newton replied, and truly, their love lasted till forever. 

It proved that love can overcome every pain and trial if only you're ready to fight for it.

                           рҹҘҖ THE END рҹҘҖ

вқӨпёҸ *RUTHLESS HUSBAND* вқӨпёҸ  
_He's the enemy, but I crave his touch_

_By:_ Charlotte  
_THEMES:_ Forbidden Love, Identity, Redemption, Trust  
_GENRE:_ Mafia Romance, Dark Romance  

*RATING:* 18+  
*CONTENT WARNINGS:* Grief, trauma, hospitalization, pregnancy, suicidal ideation implied, depression, graphic aftermath of violence, morally grey characters. Not for readers under 18.  

_Copyright:_ All rights reserved. Protected. No distribution or reproduction permitted without permission.

                 вқҘрқҗӮрқҗҮрқҗҖрқҗҸрқҗ“рқҗ„рқҗ‘ 38,39,40вқҘ  
                 *A Body Without a Soul*  
                 _Lia's POV_

I donвҖҷt remember hitting the ground.

One second I was screaming, clawing at Luke, watching fire eat the only man IвҖҷd ever loved. The next, there was justвҖҰ nothing.

Black.

 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

I woke up to beeping. White walls. Antiseptic. A hospital. 

No. No no no. I wasnвҖҷt supposed to wake up. Not without him.

I tried to sit up. My body said no. Everything hurt. My throat was raw from screaming. My hands were bandaged вҖ” IвҖҷd torn my nails off trying to get back to him.

вҖңHeвҖҷs gone.вҖқ

Luke. In a chair by the bed. Arm in a sling. Face cut up. Eyes worse. He looked like a man whoвҖҷd buried his brother.

вҖңDonвҖҷt say that,вҖқ I croaked.

вҖңLiaвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңDonвҖҷt you _dare_ say that.вҖқ I was crying before I finished the sentence. вҖңHe canвҖҷt be. He promised. He saidвҖ”вҖқ

The door opened. A doctor. Middle-aged. Tired eyes. Clipboard.

вҖңMiss lia ?вҖқ She didnвҖҷt wait for me to answer. вҖңYou had a full workup when you were brought in. Unconscious, signs of shock, dehydration. We ran bloods.вҖқ

I didnвҖҷt care. Nothing mattered.

вҖңYouвҖҷre pregnant.вҖқ

The world stopped. Again. But this time, it didnвҖҷt start back up.

вҖңApproximately six weeks,вҖқ she kept going. Gentle. Like I was made of glass. вҖңGiven the trauma, weвҖҷll want to monitor you closely. The babyвҖ”вҖқ

I didnвҖҷt hear the rest.

Six weeks. The night after my birthday. The night he told me I was the reason he lived. The night he held me and called me _solnishka_ like I could chase his darkness away.

I put my hand on my stomach. Flat. Empty. But not. 

And I _broke_.

Not loud. Not screaming. I didnвҖҷt have screams left. I just folded in half and sobbed until I couldnвҖҷt breathe. Until Luke was there, arms around me, holding me while I fell apart for the second time in two days.

вҖңHe doesnвҖҷt know,вҖқ I choked out. вҖңHe died not knowing.вҖқ

Luke didnвҖҷt say anything. Just held on tighter. 

 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

_Two Days Later_

They discharged me. No reason to keep me. Physically, I was fine. Bruised. Cut up. Pregnant. 

Alive.

Luke took me home. Not home. . ViktorвҖҷs. Our room was exactly how we left it. Bed unmade. His shirt on the floor where IвҖҷd thrown it at him, laughing, seven days ago. 

I picked it up. Held it to my face. It still smelled like him. Gunpowder and pine and something that was just _Viktor_. 

I didnвҖҷt take it off for three days.

 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

_One Week Later_

вҖңEat.вҖқ

Luke put a plate in front of me. Soup. I stared at it.

вҖңIвҖҷm not hungry.вҖқ

вҖңYou havenвҖҷt eaten in two days, Lia. The baby needsвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңI KNOW WHAT THE BABY NEEDS!вҖқ 

I didnвҖҷt mean to yell. Luke didnвҖҷt flinch. He just pushed the bowl closer.

вҖңHe made me promise, you know.вҖқ His voice was rough. вҖңRight before he shoved you at me. Said вҖҳshe eats. She sleeps. She lives. ThatвҖҷs an order.вҖҷвҖқ

I looked at him. Really looked. He had circles under his eyes. HadnвҖҷt shaved. He was grieving too. HeвҖҷd lost his brother, his boss, his best friend.

вҖңThen he shouldвҖҷve stayed alive to enforce it,вҖқ I whispered.

I ate three bites. For the baby. Not for me. I was already dead.

 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

_Three Weeks Later_

No body. 

ThatвҖҷs what Rook told me. She came by, face hard, no tears. ViktorвҖҷs people donвҖҷt cry. вҖңFire was too hot. Warehouse was rigged to burn. If he was insideвҖҰвҖқ She didnвҖҷt finish.

If he was inside, there was nothing left.

I threw up in  bathroom. Not morning sickness. Grief. They feel the same.

That night I found his jacket. The one he wore the night we met. When I was and he pulled me out of a car wreck and told his men вҖңshe pays for her father.вҖқ

I put it on. It swallowed me. Sleeves too long. Shoulders too wide. It smelled like him. Like the first time he said my name without venom.

I slept in it. Every night. 

 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

_Five Weeks Later_

вҖңPack a bag.вҖқ

Luke was in my doorway at 6 AM. вҖңWeвҖҷre leaving.вҖқ

вҖңWhere?вҖқ I didnвҖҷt care. Nowhere was safe. Nowhere was home.

Malta . I have a house. No one knows. It rarely snows вҖ” he told me you hate snow.вҖқ

I laughed. It sounded like glass breaking. вҖңHe told you that?вҖқ

вҖңNight before the warehouse. Made me swear.вҖқ Luke looked away. вҖңHe planned for this, Lia. He knew Nikolai would come back. He knew he might notвҖ”вҖқ

вҖңStop.вҖқ I couldnвҖҷt hear it. Not again.

We left that night. Private jet. Fake passports. The life Viktor built for me without me.

Malta was green. Quiet. The house was on a lake. Beautiful. 

I hated it.

 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

_Seven Weeks Later_

I talk to him. Every night.

I sit on the balcony, wearing his hoodie and his ring вҖ” I took it off his nightstand before we left вҖ” and I tell him about the baby.

вҖңTwelve weeks today,вҖқ I whispered to the dark. To the stars. To wherever he was. вҖңDoctor says itвҖҷs healthy. Strong heartbeat. Like its father.вҖқ

No answer. Never an answer.

вҖңI hate you,вҖқ I told him. вҖңFor making me love you. For making me want this. For leaving me here alone to do it.вҖқ

The wind moved. That was all.

вҖңI love you too.вҖқ I was crying again. Always crying. вҖңI shouldвҖҷve said it sooner. Before the bomb. Before your uncle. I shouldвҖҷve said it every day so youвҖҷd never doubt it.вҖқ

I put my hand on my stomach. Small bump now. Barely there. But real.

вҖңWeвҖҷre a body without a soul, Viktor. Me and the baby. WeвҖҷre alive because you told us to be. But weвҖҷre not _living_. Not without you.вҖқ

Luke found me asleep on the balcony at 3 AM. Carried me inside. DidnвҖҷt say anything. Just pulled the blanket up.

He forces me to eat. Three meals. Small. I do it for the baby. I walk because the doctor says I should. I take the vitamins. I sleep. I breathe.

ThatвҖҷs it. 

ThatвҖҷs all I am now. A checklist. Keep the baby alive. Because itвҖҷs the last piece of him left.

Rook calls every week. вҖңNo news,вҖқ she says. Same words. Every time. вҖңBut weвҖҷre still looking.вҖқ

They wonвҖҷt find him. I know it. You donвҖҷt walk away from that kind of fire.

But sometimesвҖҰ at nightвҖҰ when IвҖҷm wearing his shirt and the baby kicks for the first time, I swear I feel him. Behind me. Breath on my neck. Voice in my ear saying _solnishka_.

And for one second, IвҖҷm not a body without a soul.

IвҖҷm just a girl who loved a ruthless man. And he loved her back.

Then the second ends. And IвҖҷm alone again.

 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

_Eight Weeks Later_

I stood in front of the mirror today. Really looked. For the first time since the warehouse.

IвҖҷm pale. Thinner, except for the bump. Eyes dead. Hair long because he liked it long. Wearing his t-shirt. It says _Property of Petrov_ across the chest. He bought it as a joke. Said I was the only thing he owned that owned him back.

I touched the words. Then my stomach.

вҖңHeвҖҷd want you to fight,вҖқ I told the mirror. Told myself. Told her. вҖңHeвҖҷd want you to be mad. To be mean. To be _alive_. Not this.вҖқ

The mirror didnвҖҷt answer.

But tonight, I ate the whole bowl of soup. Luke looked surprised. DidnвҖҷt say anything. Just put a second one in front of me.

I ate half of that too. For the baby. And maybe, just a little, for him.

Because if thereвҖҷs a chance вҖ” 1% вҖ” that heвҖҷs out thereвҖҰ that heвҖҷs looking for meвҖҰ

I need to be alive when he finds me.

And if heвҖҷs notвҖҰ

Then IвҖҷll raise his child to be ruthless. To be strong. To be loved so fiercely that she never doubts it. Not once.

Like her father loved me.

TBC.........  
 М¶ М¶ М¶ М¶

CHAPTER ONE :Taken by him 


Hie guys my name is Annabel a short 18 year old girl. l grew up in a kingdom reigned by My father and stepmother .
Actually to be frank no one liked me , l was on my own .
Despite my father being the ruler , l worked as a maid ,they treated me like a piece of trash . I wore ragged clothes and slept on the maids head quarters . If l was sick no one cared for me , they said l was a bad lucky to the family because my real mother passed away giving birth to me .l endured all the pain and harsh treatments because there was no where to run to . 


I woke up early in the morning and started my usual routine but to my surprise the palace bell rang. This bell only rang when there's an emergency. We went to the summoned place only to see the royal family (my family) and their 2 daughters also present .

Dad (the king didn't waste his time he went straight to the point.

вҖңGood day, servants,вҖқ he said. вҖңI bring you exciting news. As you all know, our kingdom has suffered a terrible drought, leaving many starving. To save us, I have decided to trade my daughter Annabel to the Beast King of Alvendo in exchange for resources.вҖқ

I froze.

My heart shattered.

How could my own father sell me off like that? To *him* вҖ” the ruthless ruler of Alvendo, a kingdom feared by many. HeвҖҷs called вҖңThe Beast KingвҖқ not just because of his power, but because no one has ever seen his faceвҖҰ and those who tried never lived to tell the tale.He always wore a mask 

I had no choice. I couldnвҖҷt protest. I couldnвҖҷt run. I was nothing to them вҖ” just a sacrifice.

That afternoon, I packed what little I had. Tears silently fell down my cheeks as I climbed into the carriage headed westвҖҰ to the Alvendo Kingdom.

To the monster.

To the unknown.

To my fate.


The carriage was dark inside lined with thick 
 velvet curtains that blocked out the sun. The horses galloped steadily, their hooves pounding against the dry earth like a heartbeat counting down to my doom. I sat in silence, my hands trembling in my lap. I had no idea what awaited me in Alvendo. All I knew were the terrifying stories whispered by the palace maidsвҖ”of a king who never showed his face, who ruled with an iron fist, and who was said to have the strength of ten men and the heart of none.

I stared out the small window slit, watching as the familiar lands of my kingdom faded behind me. I wasnвҖҷt just leaving homeвҖ”I was being discarded like a broken object. My own father hadnвҖҷt even looked me in the eye when he made the announcement. My stepsisters had smirked, clearly pleased to see me go. My stepmother didnвҖҷt even pretend to hide her satisfaction.

Tears welled in my eyes, but I refused to let them fall. If I was going to be taken by a beast, I would not arrive looking weak.

After hours of travel, the landscape began to change. The skies darkened unnaturally, and the air grew colder. Trees stood taller here, their twisted branches clawing at the sky like skeletal fingers. The road narrowed, flanked by black stones and eerie silence. Even the birds had stopped singing.

The carriage came to a halt before a massive iron gate, adorned with the crest of a roaring lion and a serpent coiled around a sword. The gates creaked open slowly, revealing a long path lined with torches that flickered with blue flames. At the end of the path stood a castle unlike any I had ever seenвҖ”tall, shadowy, and carved from obsidian stone. It looked like it had been plucked from a nightmare.

A tall figure awaited me at the entrance. He was cloaked in black, his face hidden beneath a hood. I couldnвҖҷt see his eyes, but I could feel themвҖ”cold, calculating, and ancient.

вҖңAnnabel,вҖқ he said, his voice deep and smooth like velvet over steel. вҖңWelcome to Alvendo.вҖқ

I swallowed hard. вҖңAre youвҖҰ the Beast King?вҖқ
I expected to see horns on his head like the rumors but there was completely nothing . I expected to see fangs and him smelling like blood but it was actually the opposite

He didnвҖҷt answer. Instead, he turned and began walking into the castle. Two guards flanked me, urging me to follow. My legs moved on their own, though every step felt like I was walking deeper into a trap.

Inside, the castle was both beautiful and terrifying. The walls shimmered with dark crystal, and the chandeliers above glowed with floating orbs of light. Statues of creatures I couldnвҖҷt name lined the hallsвҖ”some with wings, others with horns, all with eyes that seemed to follow me.

I was led to a grand chamber with a high ceiling and a throne carved from bone and gold. The cloaked man stood beside it, but he did not sit.

вҖңYou belong to me now,вҖқ he said. вҖңYou will follow my rules. You will not leave this castle without permission. And you will neverвҖ”under any circumstancesвҖ”enter the East Wing.вҖқAnd one more thing you're going to be my wife .They will be wedding preparation whether you like it or not l don't care, it's just for my benefit after that l will dump you .

I opened my mouth to protest, but his voice cut through me like a blade.

вҖңSpeak only when spoken to.вҖқ

I clenched my fists. I had been treated like a servant my whole life, but thisвҖҰ this was something else. I was a prisoner now. A pawn in a game I didnвҖҷt understand.

But deep inside, a fire began to burn. I didnвҖҷt know what fate awaited me here, but I swore to myselfвҖ”I would not break. I would not be tamed.

Not even by the Beast King.

---

рҹ©ё *BLOOD OATH* рҹ—ЎпёҸ  
{The DeVane Syndicate & I}

*RATING:* 18+  

*CONTENT WARNINGS:* Graphic violence, blood, gore, torture mentions, knife violence, gun violence, injury detail, supernatural horror, death, morally grey protagonists, possessive behavior. Not for readers under 18.

By,  
    Charlotte вңҚпёҸ

DO NOT COPY OR REPOST вҡ пёҸ

                 вқҘрқҗӮрқҗҮрқҗҖрқҗҸрқҗ“рқҗ„рқҗ‘ рқҹ•вқҘ  
                 *Blood for Blood*  
                 _Dante's POV_

Twenty-six days left.

She ate. Barely. Toast and water, because anything else made her throw up. The Patron didnвҖҷt like food. Said it _dulled_ her. Made her less _bright_.

I didnвҖҷt care. I sat across from her at 4 AM and watched her choke down every bite like it was a personal war. Every swallow, the silver in my grandfatherвҖҷs veins dimmed a fraction. Good. Starve it, even a little.

вҖңYouвҖҷre staring,вҖқ she said. Voice like paper.

вҖңYouвҖҷre breathing. IвҖҷll stare if I want.вҖқ 

The mark on her ankle from the altar was black now. Matching the one on her palm. They pulsed in time with my heartbeat. Or maybe mine pulsed with hers. CouldnвҖҷt tell anymore.

RookвҖҷs text came at 5:03 AM: _Russo car outside north house. LilaвҖҷs location is blown._

I was moving before I finished reading. Gun, knife, jacket. Wren was up too. SheвҖҷd seen my face.

вҖңLila?вҖқ

вҖңSheвҖҷs safe. Rook moved her.вҖқ Lie. The mark on WrenвҖҷs palm flared. She gasped. I did too. Felt like a nail through my ribs. вҖңStop asking me that.вҖқ

вҖңYouвҖҷre lying.вҖқ

вҖңTo keep you here.вҖқ I grabbed her shoulders. вҖңIf you run out there, Marco wins. He uses you to get to me. He uses _me_ to get to the merge. You understand?вҖқ

вҖңSo we just leave her?вҖқ

вҖңWe donвҖҷt.вҖқ I let go. вҖң_I_ donвҖҷt. You stay. In this house. In the warded rooms. You do not bleed. You do not pray. You do not _think_ his name.вҖқ

вҖңAnd if you die?вҖқ

вҖңThen the Patron takes you on day twenty-six instead of day zero. Same ending.вҖқ I was already at the door. вҖңSo donвҖҷt let me die.вҖқ

The north house was a smoking hole by the time I got there. Not burned. _Melted_. That was RussoвҖҷs Patron. It liked fire. Liked screaming.

Rook was in the street, arm bleeding, gun empty. вҖңThey took her. Twenty minutes ago. Marco left a message.вҖқ

She held up a phone. Video playing. Lila, zip-tied to a chair, mouth taped. Alive. Crying. Behind her, Marco. Smiling.

вҖңTell the Reaper,вҖқ he said to the camera. вҖңTradeвҖҷs simple. His life-mate for the sister. SheвҖҷs Russo blood anyway. Belongs with us. He has one hour before I see if Russo blood and DeVane blood mix.вҖқ He lifted a knife. Not to LilaвҖҷs throat. To her palm. вҖңWeвҖҷll start with a matching set.вҖқ

The video ended.

The Patron in me went _silent_. That was worse than screaming. Silence meant it was deciding.

вҖңWhere?вҖқ I asked Rook.

вҖңSt. Louis Cathedral. Basement. Old Russo territory. He wants you on holy ground. Thinks itвҖҷll weaken ours.вҖқ

It wouldnвҖҷt. Our Patron was older than their god.

I left Rook bleeding in the street. Called cleanup on the way. DidnвҖҷt look back.

The cathedral at 6 AM was empty. Except for the ghosts. The Patrons loved churches. So many dead, so much begging. Buffet.

Marco was in the crypt. Lila in the chair. Knife already bloody. Her palm was cut, same symbol as WrenвҖҷs, carved shallow. _Matching set_.

вҖңYouвҖҷre late,вҖқ Marco said. He looked tired. Using his Patron too much did that. Ate you from the inside. вҖңI thought youвҖҷd care more.вҖқ

вҖңLet her go.вҖқ

вҖңNo.вҖқ He tilted his head. вҖңBut IвҖҷll trade. You for her. Your PatronвҖҷs stronger. Put _you_ on my altar, and mine eats well for a decade.вҖқ

вҖңYou donвҖҷt want my Patron. You want the merge.вҖқ

вҖңSmart boy.вҖқ He grinned. вҖңYour little vessel told you? SheвҖҷs ours, Dante. Russo blood. YouвҖҷre just borrowing. Give her back, and maybe I let the sister live.вҖқ

The Patron in my chest was _laughing_. _He doesnвҖҷt know. He doesnвҖҷt know sheвҖҷs already claimed. He doesnвҖҷt know she bled for you._

I felt it then. Sharp, sudden, like a rib breaking. Not me. _Her_.

Wren.

Twenty miles away, she was bleeding. I knew it like I knew my own name. The mark on my palm was wet. The one on my ankle burned.

She wasnвҖҷt supposed to bleed. She was supposed to stay in the warded rooms.

Marco saw my face change. вҖңWhatвҖ”вҖқ

The cathedral doors blew inward.

Wren stood there. Barefoot. Nightgown. Kitchen knife in her hand, bloody. Her palm was open, dripping on the stone. The ghosts in the cathedral were _screaming_, all of them, pointing at her.

вҖңI told you not to come,вҖқ I said. CouldnвҖҷt breathe.

вҖңHe has Lila.вҖқ Her eyes were all black. Not the Patron. _Hers_. Rage. вҖңYou think I was gonna stay home?вҖқ

Marco started laughing. вҖңPerfect. You brought her yourself. Elena, you should see this. Your niece, all grown up and stupid.вҖқ

He moved toward Wren. I moved faster. But she was faster than both of us.

She didnвҖҷt go for Marco. She went for _me_. Slammed her bleeding palm to my chest, right over my heart. 

The world went white.

Pact law was simple: a life-mate claim required blood freely given, a true name, and intent. SheвҖҷd already done two. SheвҖҷd said my name the night she did the ritual. SheвҖҷd bled for me then.

Now she was giving intent.

вҖңI choose you,вҖқ she said. Not to me. To the _floor_. To _Him_. вҖңNot the Patron. Not the merge. Him. Dante DeVane. I claim him.вҖқ

The symbol on her palm _burned_ into my skin. Through my shirt. Through bone. I felt it brand itself on my heart. The Patron _howled_. Not in rage. In _triumph_.

_CLAIMED_, the cathedral said. Every ghost said it. The stones said it. _CLAIMED CLAIMED CLAIMED_.

Marco stopped. вҖңNo. No, thatвҖҷs notвҖ”she canвҖҷtвҖ”sheвҖҷs _mine_вҖ”вҖқ

вҖңSheвҖҷs mine,вҖқ I said. And it was true now. Final. Law. The black in WrenвҖҷs eyes receded. The bleeding stopped. The mark on my chest was permanent.

Lila screamed behind the tape. Marco had the knife to her throat now. вҖңThen she watches her sister die. And you get to live with it for twenty-six more days.вҖқ

Wren looked at me. No fear. Just _certainty_. And she nodded.

I knew what she was saying. _He chooses._

I moved. The Patron didnвҖҷt need to ride me. DidnвҖҷt need to drink. This was _me_. I covered the distance, took MarcoвҖҷs knife hand, and _broke_ it. Bones. Tendons. All of it. He screamed.

The knife fell. I caught it. Put it to his throat.

вҖңPact says new contracts are fair game,вҖқ I told him. My voice wasnвҖҷt mine. вҖңPact also says you touch whatвҖҷs mine, I take your hands. And your throat. And your Patron.вҖқ

I cut. Not deep. Not a kill. A _message_. For his Patron. 

Blood hit the stone. The cathedral _drank_ it. MarcoвҖҷs Patron screamed, somewhere far away and underground.

вҖңGet out,вҖқ I told him. вҖңTell your Patron the merge is off the table. Tell it the bridge is burned. Tell it if I see you in my city again, IвҖҷll finish what my mother started and burn your bloodline out by the root.вҖқ

He ran. Holding his throat, broken hand cradled to his chest.

I cut Lila free. She collapsed into me, sobbing. Alive. Whole. Minus one scar on her palm that would never fade.

Wren was still standing by the door. Waiting. The ghosts were gone. The cathedral was just stone again.

I walked to her. Lifted her chin. The mark on my chest burned. The ones on her palm and ankle answered.

вҖңYou claimed me,вҖқ I said.

вҖңYou were gonna die for her.вҖқ She wasnвҖҷt sorry. вҖңIвҖҷm not letting the Patron use that against you.вҖқ

вҖңYou know what this means?вҖқ

вҖңYeah.вҖқ She touched the spot over my heart. Where her name was now. вҖңIt means IвҖҷm yours until death. And in this familyвҖҰвҖқ

вҖңThatвҖҷs where it starts,вҖқ I finished.

Outside, the sun was up. Day twenty-six. 

SheвҖҷd just cut our time down to zero. No more countdown. No more waiting. 

The Patron was settled. Fed. _Happy_. Because now it had what it wanted: a DeVane and a Russo, bound by blood, by choice.

The merge didnвҖҷt need her body. It just needed the claim.

And sheвҖҷd given it to me.

LETHABO 

When I was  growing up, my upcoming wasn't all so rosey. Growing up was survival to me, it wasn't about living the best but learning how worst it can get in life, my life had been revolving around abuse, bullying, unfair treatment and manipulation, but I still lived maybe by God's grace.

I grew up in hands of relatives who cared because of I was a hard worker but some didn't care because I finished their groceries, not representative and an ugly creator, that I was dark and skinny with no parents, but atleast I was obedient. 

From the age of 2 moved from one relative to another, from gogo to malume, malume to rakgadi and from there to distance relatives. Till now my life revolves around packing my two pair of clothes, shirt and skin Jean that doesn't even reach my вҖ”вҖ”anymore  and a plastic back carrying only my sandals. And oh hunny that's was how awful my life started. 

Well not really I mean___No, hunny I had parents and I still do but they didn't see me, they didn't even feel my presence the minute I shine they will try by means to break me apart my smile was their sorrows, my Dad didn't even look back when I had just  turned just 2 years, they said he left to work in the mines but he never came back for me, I was a nobody, even though I was his first little princess, to him girls child didn't matter.

OH  and my mom? she sometimes acted so holy but mtase she wasn't she just wanted to feel good, but then I understand hey, she is still blind by heartache. 

Okay enough about my parents but let me take you through my journey of life, call me LETHABO , but in the journey we are about to approach call me Dikeledi or KEFENTSE, because we'll that's what I did, cried and pulled through and also dodged bullets. 

This is the story of my life baby,hop in.

Hey sweetpie, I'm Lethabo Moeti, a thin brownskinned girly aged 20 years of age, a mother and a survivor.

Will start this novel on Sunday morning or Saturday night, you will get two chapters a day

My dropping schedule will be like :
-Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday and Saturday.

TWO chapters a day only if the target is reached
#writer #realityoflife

[ *V3.0.0 - REDESIGN UI&UX STATUS PAGE* ]

Hello developer, saat ini kami telah menambahkan status page agar kalian dapat mengetahui sistem kami down dimana aja. Sistem ini memakai websocket dan bukan api polling agar server tidak menjadi berat, kami akan kasih laina update kembali secepat nya. Terimakasih atas antusiasme anda

message.conversation:
[ *PEMBERITAHUAN* ]

Hello developer, saat ini kami sedang mengalami masalah serius tentang *V3.0.0*, hal ini bukan masalah kecil tapi masalah besar. File *V3.0.0*  tidak sengaja terhapus akibat ke cerobohan kami, namun kami pastikan *V3.0.0* tetap kelar dihari sabtu namun dengan catatan kami tidak bisa 100% mengembalikan fungsi nya. Kami saat ini memiliki backup namun backup ini hanya sebatas cadangan karna yang sudah 100% jadi tidak sengaja terkena "SUDO RM -RF", tapi kami sedang berusaha memulihkan folder penting yang akan digabung dengan backup. Sekali lagi kami meminta maaf atas kelalaian kami dalam membuat rest api
